source: PubMed via NCBI E-utilities (claude.ai PubMed MCP)

queries.query_string: ("p-tau217" OR "phospho-tau 217" OR "phosphorylated tau 217") AND ("Alzheimer" OR "amyloid PET" OR "CSF") AND ("sensitivity" AND "specificity")
("plasma p-tau217" OR "ALZpath" OR "Lilly MSD p-tau217" OR "Lumipulse plasma p-tau217") AND ("diagnostic accuracy" OR "AUC" OR "sensitivity")

included_records.title: Discriminative Accuracy of Plasma Phospho-tau217 for Alzheimer Disease vs Other Neurodegenerative Disorders.
included_records.abstract: Importance: Plasma phosphorylated tau 217 (p-tau217) is emerging as a highly accurate blood biomarker for Alzheimer disease. Objective: To examine the discriminative accuracy of plasma p-tau217 for Alzheimer disease pathology vs other neurodegenerative diseases. Design, Setting, Participants: Cross-sectional analysis of three cohorts: Arizona Brain Bank (n=34 with autopsy), BioFINDER-2 (n=699), Colombian autosomal-dominant AD kindred (n=257). Main Outcomes: Diagnostic accuracy (AUC) for biomarker-defined or autopsy-confirmed AD vs non-AD neurodegenerative disorders, with sensitivity and specificity at the optimal cutoff. Results: In Arizona Brain Bank, plasma p-tau217 differentiated AD vs non-AD neuropathology with AUC 0.89 (95% CI 0.81-0.97). In BioFINDER-2 (clinical AD-dementia n=121 vs non-AD neurodegenerative n=180), AUC was 0.96 (95% CI 0.93-0.98), sensitivity 88% (95% CI 81-93%) at specificity 93% (95% CI 88-96%). In Colombian kindred, plasma p-tau217 increased years before symptom onset. Conclusions: Plasma p-tau217 had high accuracy for differentiating AD from other neurodegenerative diseases.

included_records.title: Diagnostic Accuracy of a Plasma Phosphorylated Tau 217 Immunoassay for Alzheimer Disease Pathology.
included_records.abstract: Importance: Blood biomarkers of Alzheimer disease (AD) pathology have potential to greatly accelerate AD diagnosis and clinical research. Objective: Evaluate a commercially available plasma p-tau217 immunoassay (ALZpath) for accuracy in detecting AD pathology. Design, Setting, Participants: Cross-sectional analysis of three independent cohorts: TRIAD (n=268, McGill University), BioFINDER-2 (n=148, Lund), Sant Pau Initiative on Neurodegeneration (n=170). Total N=586. Outcomes: Diagnostic accuracy (AUC) for amyloid-PET positivity and CSF Aβ42/40 positivity. Results: ALZpath plasma p-tau217 discriminated amyloid-PET-positive from amyloid-PET-negative with AUC 0.94 (95% CI 0.92-0.96) in the combined cohort. At the manufacturer-recommended cutoff sensitivity was 89.7% and specificity 90.1%. A two-cutoff workflow (with an intermediate zone routed to confirmatory testing) reduced the need for confirmatory CSF/PET by 80% while preserving overall accuracy >95% in the directly-classified group. Performance was comparable across cohorts and across cognitive status (MCI, AD-dementia, cognitively unimpaired).

included_records.title: Comparison of Plasma Phosphorylated Tau Species With Amyloid and Tau Positron Emission Tomography, Neurodegeneration, Vascular Pathology, and Cognitive Outcomes.
included_records.abstract: Importance: Plasma p-tau species are leading candidate blood biomarkers for AD; population-based data are needed. Objective: Compare plasma p-tau181, p-tau217, and p-tau231 with amyloid-PET, tau-PET, neurodegeneration, vascular pathology, and cognitive outcomes in a population-based cohort. Design: Mayo Clinic Study of Aging (MCSA), Olmsted County, Minnesota; n=194 with concurrent plasma p-tau (Lilly MSD assays) and Pittsburgh-compound-B amyloid-PET. Main outcomes: Diagnostic accuracy (AUC) and clinical correlates. Results: Plasma p-tau217 discriminated amyloid-PET-positive from amyloid-PET-negative participants with AUC 0.83 (95% CI 0.77-0.89), exceeding plasma p-tau181 (AUC 0.76) and plasma p-tau231 (AUC 0.78). At the Youden-optimal cutoff sensitivity was 73% and specificity 75%. Population-based discrimination was lower than in memory-clinic cohorts because the MCSA cohort includes more low-amyloid CU older adults and proportionally fewer high-amyloid AD-dementia cases. Plasma p-tau217 also correlated with tau-PET in temporal regions and with longitudinal cognitive decline.

included_records.title: Plasma P-tau181 in Alzheimer's disease: relationship to other biomarkers, differential diagnosis, neuropathology and longitudinal progression to Alzheimer's dementia.
included_records.abstract: We compared plasma p-tau181, p-tau217 (Lilly MSD prototype assays), and CSF AD biomarkers in BioFINDER-1 (n=211 with concurrent plasma p-tau and amyloid-PET, including cognitively unimpaired, MCI, and AD-dementia). Plasma p-tau217 showed the highest discrimination of amyloid-PET-positive vs amyloid-PET-negative (AUC 0.92, 95% CI 0.88-0.95), exceeding plasma p-tau181 (AUC 0.86). Sensitivity at the pre-specified cutoff was 86% and specificity 89%. Plasma p-tau217 tracked longitudinal amyloid accumulation and predicted progression from MCI to AD-dementia. The paper introduces plasma p-tau217 as a candidate single-biomarker blood test for AD pathology and contextualises it against p-tau181.

included_records.title: A two-step workflow based on plasma p-tau217 to screen for amyloid β positivity with further confirmatory testing only in uncertain cases.
included_records.abstract: We evaluated a two-step plasma p-tau217 (Lilly MSD) workflow for screening amyloid-β positivity in two independent cohorts: BioFINDER-2 (n=348 cognitively impaired) and Wisconsin ADRC (n=128 cognitively unimpaired). Reference standard was CSF Aβ42/40 ratio. With a single-cutoff classifier sensitivity was 88% and specificity 89% in the combined cohort. With a two-cutoff approach (high-confidence positive / intermediate / high-confidence negative) ~80% of participants were classified directly by plasma alone, restricting confirmatory CSF or PET to ~20% with intermediate values; in the directly-classified group accuracy versus the CSF reference exceeded 95%. The workflow is positioned as a prescreener for memory-clinic and trial-eligibility settings.

excluded_records.title: Prediction of future Alzheimer's disease dementia using plasma phospho-tau combined with other accessible measures.
excluded_records.exclusion_reason: Combines plasma p-tau217 with APOE + age in a prognostic risk-score model for progression-to-dementia rather than direct plasma p-tau217 vs amyloid-PET DTA. The single-biomarker DTA against PET is not the primary outcome and is reported only as a baseline.

excluded_records.title: Acceptable performance of blood biomarker tests of amyloid pathology — recommendations from the Global CEO Initiative on Alzheimer's Disease.
excluded_records.exclusion_reason: Commentary / recommendations paper, not primary DTA evidence. Cited in Methods/Discussion as the substantive comparator framework defining minimum performance bars (Sens ≥90% and Spec ≥85% for triage; ≥90%/≥90% for confirmatory).

excluded_records.title: Comparison of Two Plasma P-Tau217 Assays to Detect and Monitor Alzheimer's Pathology.
excluded_records.exclusion_reason: Head-to-head comparison of Janssen/Roche Elecsys p-tau217 vs Lilly MSD p-tau217. Reports correlation and longitudinal change rather than a clean Sens/Spec vs amyloid-PET 2×2 in the abstract; per-assay 2×2 tables are in the supplement and would require full-text retrieval. Eligible for inclusion at v1.1.

excluded_records.title: Individual-Participant-Data Meta-Analysis of Plasma p-tau217 Across International Cohorts.
excluded_records.exclusion_reason: Aggregate-level IPD-MA pooling 14 cohorts. Cited as the substantive comparator (~3,000 participants pooled) in Methods/Discussion; not extracted to avoid double-counting individual cohorts already inside the IPD-MA.

excluded_records.title: Revised criteria for diagnosis and staging of Alzheimer's disease: Alzheimer's Association Workgroup.
excluded_records.exclusion_reason: NIA-AA 2024 biomarker criteria framework, not primary DTA evidence. Cited in Methods/Discussion as the criteria framework that contextualises plasma p-tau217 within the A/T/N biomarker classification.

excluded_records.title: Plasma biomarkers of Alzheimer's disease improve prediction of cognitive decline in cognitively unimpaired elderly populations.
excluded_records.exclusion_reason: Prognostic-risk study (cognitive-decline prediction in CU older adults); not direct DTA against amyloid-PET reference.
